rationale: The citing paper repeatedly uses Lee and Labroo (2004) to justify its core fluency-based mechanism in the hypothesis development section. It cites that work for the conceptual vs. perceptual fluency distinction and for the claim that fluency shapes evaluative judgments, then builds predictions that descriptive logos are easier to process and affect brand outcomes. Although the citation appears with other fluency references and is not used for direct method reuse, it meaningfully informs the paper's conceptual setup and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Lee and Labroo's fluency framework to logo design, arguing that higher logo descriptiveness increases conceptual fluency and thereby influences authenticity perceptions and downstream brand evaluations. In this sense, the cited paper provides the fluency logic transferred to a new branding context.
